plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-540 RP17.P07.M02.S13-RP17.P07.M04.S05 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-540 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP17.P07 implementation slice: the M02 Type And Shape Definition tail (the ten remaining rows), the full M03 Primary Implementation Slice cycle (twenty-five rows), and the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice head (the first five rows) as three descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H17), Claude review packet (C17), and CP00-541 handoff, without opening AI governance, model policy, retrieval scope, or citation runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-540 is a descriptor-only Risk B pack (RP17.P07 implementation slice, 40 units, RP17.P07.M02.S13-RP17.P07.M04.S05) that correctly claims production_ready=false, no_real_data=true, writes_product_state=false, and defers all runtime opening to CP00-541 under required human approval. The pack-level claim (implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout + descriptor-verified flag) is fully supported by inspection: the CP540 pack binding, no-write attestation, and requirements (registry.js:443-458, 2248-2303) are consistent with the descriptor/case-set builders (service.js:3337-3408) and the coverage/slice/Hermes/Claude/handoff validators (validators.js:4752-4919). Deliverable distribution {failure_recovery:18, implementation:7, hermes_evidence:4, fixture:2, test:4, security_audit:3, claude_review:2} and micro-title distribution {Type And Shape Definition:10, Primary Implementation Slice:25, Secondary Workflow Slice:5} match the prompt baseline, the registry requirements, and the live closeout-pack-plan entry (closeout-pack-plan.json:407). All 7 required exports exist and are surfaced via index.js wildcard re-exports; the README CP00-540 section accurately describes the slice. Boundaries are solid: no fs/network/child_process anywhere in packages/ai-governance/src; freezeCp540Result stamps a comprehensive no-write/no-runtime/no-leak attestation set (service.js:3289-3331) mirrored in the contract projection (ai-governance-core-contract.json:26494-26530); the validator deep-equals the contract's p07_implementation_slice_descriptor against the freshly generated descriptor (validate-rp17...mjs:1439-1443). Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not final approver (claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false), and Hermes control-plane patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, receipt_shape descriptor_only_governance_p07_implementation_slice_no_write). CP178+ review hardening is encoded (invalid_review_blockers include failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, missing fields) and the readiness auth-preflight passed with non-zero stdout. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly still pending. One non-blocking P3: changed-file-scope.json double-lists two untracked files in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved. No P0/P1/P2 issues; does not block pack or goal closeout, and human final approval for any enterprise trust claim remains required.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies two untracked files as both pack-implementation and unrelated-preserved
findings.details: The generated scope artifact lists docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-540/ in untracked_implementation_files (lines 151-154) and ALSO in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 156-169). These two classifications are mutually exclusive in intent. The pack manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json:1719-1731) excludes both of these (it correctly treats the pack's own docs/closeout-packs/cp00-540/ directory as pack-related), so the two generated artifacts disagree on the categorization. active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty and active_pack_required_untracked_files is empty, so no required file is dropped and the effective pack scope (8 active implementation refs) is unaffected. This is a cosmetic inconsistency in review tooling metadata, not in any pack deliverable, and has no functional impact on the descriptor-only boundaries or the production_ready=false claim.
findings.recommendation: Align the scope generator so a file appears in only one of untracked_implementation_files or unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, matching the manifest's worktree_transition_policy categorization (treat docs/closeout-packs/cp00-540/ as pack-related). No change required to pack source, tests, or contract.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking informational. Does not affect pack scope determination, boundaries, tests, or closeout gating; recommend the Codex side correct the generator output in a future tooling pass.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-540 ai governance core p07 implementation slice descriptor pack
